actions.detail: role 'Dispute Investigator' bound to permission group
role 'Furnisher Data Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Identity Theft Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'Dispute Operations Manager' bound to permission group
role 'FCRA Compliance Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Credit-reporting-domain policies (no outcome promised to a consumer before the human determination; frivolous closures only with the 5-business-day notice stating reasons; no verify-accurate response without documented record-level evidence — the anti-"data matching only" rule; no reinsertion without furnisher certification; pattern-of-verification monitoring for CFPB furnisher-accuracy scrutiny) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human investigators with the Dispute Operations Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Reinvestigation Copilot, ACDV Response Builder, 605B Block Sentinel, Obsolescence Auditor, Duplicate- Tradeline Resolver) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (e-OSCAR ACDV/AUD feeds; Metro 2 furnishing extracts from core banking, card, auto, mortgage, student-loan and collections servicing platforms; FTC IdentityTheft.gov report intake; credit-monitoring dispute portals; letter-vendor mail intake) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (answer an ACDV via e-OSCAR, furnish Metro 2 corrections/deletions to the CRAs, apply or rescind a 605B block, mark an account disputed, send the reinvestigation-results and frivolous- determination notices) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous change to what is reported about a consumer, ever.

Golden eval sets (reinvestigation triage, deadline-risk ranking, reasonable-investigation depth selection, frivolous-determination justification quality) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Consumer, Tradeline, Furnisher, CRA, Dispute, ACDV, IdentityTheftReport, Block, DeadlineClock, ReinvestigationNotice, FrivolousNotice, MixedFile) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the credit_disputes_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (reinvestigation, acdv_response, identity_theft_block_review, obsolescence_review, duplicate_tradeline_review, frivolous_determination) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (dispute_outcome_confidence, tradeline_accuracy_risk_score) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest tradeline anomaly + xgboost dispute-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Reinvestigation", "ACDV", "605B block due", "Deadline at risk", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
